items.title: Commanders WR Luke McCaffrey is 'stronger and faster' in 2026
items.summary: Ed McCaffrey played 13 seasons in the NFL, winning three Super Bowls. He believes son Luke is 'stronger and faster' in 2026.

items.title: Next step for Colts’ Tyler Warren: More
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – There’s always more, or at least the anticipation of more. So, yes, more from Tyler Warren. And the 14th overall pick in the 2025 NFL Draft set his personal bar rather high. Coach Shane Steichen offered a wry grin when asked whether Warren 2.0 could be an even bigger X-factor in 2026. “Well, I [...]

items.title: CBS Sports believes Colts ‘best-and-worst case outcomes’ both involve QB Daniel Jones
items.summary: According to CBS Sports’ Tyler Sullivan, the Indianapolis Cotls ‘best-and-worst-case outcomes’ both involve starting quarterback Daniel Jones: Indianapolis Colts Best-case: Daniel Jones is ready to roll for Week 1 despite suffering an Achilles tear last season, and the Colts quarterback picks up where he left off. Jones continues to enjoy his late-career revival in Indy and has the team […]

items.title: PFF lists Colts Shane Steichen among ‘6 NFL head coaches on hot seat’
items.summary: According to PFF, Indianapolis Colts head coach Shane Steichen is ‘among the league’s six head coaches currently on the hot seat ahead of the upcoming 2026 campaign’: Shane Steichen, Indianapolis Colts Like other names on this list, it was fairly shocking that Indianapolis retained both Steichen and general manager Chris Ballard this offseason. Over his three years […]

items.title: Colts Blue Zone Podcast 470: 2026 Indianapolis Colts True or False

items.title: Sauce Gardner’s unlucky streak could continue with the Colts to start 2026
items.summary: Sauce Gardner is great. He is one of the best cornerbacks in the league. Whether he is the best is up for debate. So too is the decision to trade two first-round picks for him. Those are the conversations that make the world of sports move. What isn’t up for debate is his ability on […]

items.title: Goals for Colts’ CB Sauce Gardner include All-Pro, Pro Bowl, first playoff appearance
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The trade was blockbuster-level and accompanied by stratospheric expectations. Sending two first-round picks plus young wideout AD Mitchell to the New York Jets for Sauce Gardner on Nov. 4 sent shockwaves across the NFL landscape and instantly injected a two-time All-Pro cornerback into Lou Anarumo’s defense. Anarumo’s secondary had been boosted eight months earlier when [...]

items.title: Blue named NFL 'Mascot of the Year,' earning back-to-back honors for the Colts
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — For the second year in a row, the best mascot in professional football resides in the Circle City. The NFL announced its Mascot of the Year award on Tuesday, and the winner was none other than Blue. The face of the Indianapolis Colts, Blue has now won the award five times. This is [...]

items.title: Blue named NFL Mascot of the Year, earns back-to-back honors
items.summary: Created in 2006, this is Blue's fifth time winning the award and second year in a row taking it home.

items.title: Could the Colts be in the market to bolster their receiver room?
items.summary: Michael Pittman Jr. is gone. He was traded by the Indianapolis Colts to the Pittsburgh Steelers. It was a tough decision, but given the big time money the Colts were handing out to Alec Pierce and Daniel Jones, it had to be done. Unfortunately, Pierce is out for at least another month and maybe more. […]

items.title: Colts Reacts Survey Results: Offseason Blues

items.title: Three Potential Players the Colts Could Add Before Week 1
items.summary: The Colts spent this offseason implementing the Seahawks method of roster construction. Retaining one side of the ball, while making wholesale changes on the other in order to get younger and to fit the scheme of the new coordinator. However, there are still holes on this roster. Not 2024 secondary sized holes, but significant holes […]

items.title: Alec Pierce’s absence should make the Colts fidget in their seats
items.summary: Before the ink was dry on a brand new $114 million deal, Alec Pierce found himself undergoing ankle surgery which carried a 4-6 month timetable. This isn’t to imply that anything was calculated or malicious, but that was some bad timing because that’s a lot of time on the shelf. The hope was to avoid […]

items.title: Issues follow Colts into offseason, into training camp
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Eight weeks of concentrated offseason work didn’t reveal issues for the Indianapolis Colts. It reinforced them. The team began its offseason regimen April 20 and wrapped things up Thursday with several pressing topics heading into a consequential 2026 season. No new areas surfaced, but there’s no denying the Colts will head to Grand [...]

items.title: When will you know that the Colts are in for a good season?
items.summary: I think the earliest sign that we may be at minimum competitive, is seeing Daniel Jones play a good quarter of football in the preseason. I’d actually be okay with two good series. But just because it is the earliest sign, will his health be the single most important indicator of a successful season? Besides, […]

A big-guys’ kicking contest to cap the three-day mandatory minicamp featured the 325-pound veteran guard, 307-pound guard Dalton Tucker, 304-pound defensive tackle Cody Wooden, 264-pound Jaylayn Tuimoloau, assistant offensive line coach Chris Watt and assistant defensive [...]

items.title: Quenton Nelson is now the veteran that young players look up to
items.summary: Nelson was drafted in the first round by the Colts in 2018. The three-time AP First Team All-Pro guard is now a locker room leader, especially on the offensive line.

items.title: Jonathan Taylor: ‘Definitely love to be a Colt for life’
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The productivity has been undeniable. Now, Jonathan Taylor would like to supplement that with longevity. One of the NFL’s premier running backs is entering the final year of his contract for a second time in four seasons, and his goal is simple. “Oh, I’d definitely love to be a Colt for life,’’ Taylor [...]

items.title: Return for Colts’ Alec Pierce likely to be mid-to-late training camp
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Alec Pierce’s return from ankle surgery remains a moving target. The Indianapolis Colts’ veteran receiver, who signed a four-year, $114 million contract in March that made him one of the NFL’s highest-paid players at his position, has been a spectator during the team’s offseason workouts. That includes the ongoing mandatory minicamp. Pierce underwent [...]

items.title: Colts WR Alec Pierce explains ankle surgery, recovery timeline, status for 2026 training camp
items.summary: Pierce underwent a procedure this spring after a PRP injection didn't alleviate an ankle issue he'd been dealing with throughout 2025.

items.title: Daniel Jones' Achilles' recovery remains on track, which has major implications for Colts' 2026 season
items.summary: Jones spent the last two weeks of the Colts' offseason program participating in seven-on-seven portions of practice, and should be ready for not only Week 1, but for the start of training camp.

items.title: Colts expecting (needing) breakout third season from Laiatu Latu

The Indianapolis Colts’ 2024 first-round draft pick – the first defensive player taken with the 15th overall selection – must be the tip of the spear for coordinator Lou Anarumo’s pass rush. And Latu knows [...]

items.title: Colts sign fourth-round picks Jalen Farmer, Bryce Boettcher
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Indianapolis Colts’ latest draft class is signed, sealed and delivered. The team signed its two remaining unsigned picks, both selected in the fourth round of the April draft. Guard Jalen Farmer signed a four-year deal worth a projected $5.591 million with a $1.21 million signing bonus, according to overthecap.com while linebacker Bryce [...]

items.title: Another week, more progress for Colts’ Daniel Jones
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Daniel Jones’ rehab continued to progress without a hiccup. The Indianapolis Colts’ starting quarterback saw his first action in 7-on-7 work during Monday’s organized team activities (OTAs) practice. That was the next anticipated step as Jones recovers from Dec. 9 surgery to repair a torn right Achilles tendon. Prior to Monday, Jones’ participation [...]

items.summary: Jones is nearing the six-month mark from sustaining a torn Achilles against the Jacksonville Jaguars in Week 14 of the 2025 season.

items.title: Colts Hall of Famer Raymond Berry dies
items.summary: Berry spent all 13 years of his NFL career with the Baltimore Colts and was inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame in 1973.

items.title: Colts legend, Hall of Famer Raymond Berry dies at 93
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Colts and NFL have lost a legend and a gentleman. Raymond Berry, a trail-blazing receiver with the Baltimore Colts, has died. He was 93. Berry’s family made the announcement Monday in a message to the Pro Football Hall of Fame. Berry was a two-time world champion with the Colts and teamed with [...]

items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — Until informed otherwise, Anthony Richardson Sr. is going about the business of competing with Riley Leonard as Daniel Jones’ backup. Any vision issues from a fractured right orbit bone have subsided — "They cleared me last year, so everything is smooth,’’ he insisted — and the Indianapolis Colts’ erstwhile quarterback of the future [...]

When he’s not bouncing on his feet while moving laterally over pads on the turf, he’s taking his reps and throwing darts to receivers. After [...]

items.title: Start time for August 29 Detroit preseason game moved to 1 p.m.
items.summary: The start time of the Indianapolis Colts’ Aug. 29 preseason matchup vs. the Detroit Lions has been moved to 1 p.m. ET.  The original start time was 3 p.m. The game will be broadcast locally on CBS4.
